Rust · Python · WebAssembly

ringgrid

Coded ring target detection in Rust. ringgrid finds dense rings on a hex lattice, fits each marker's inner and outer ellipses to sub-pixel precision, decodes its 16-sector ID against an 893-word codebook, and recovers the board-to-image homography. Partial views still decode to absolute IDs — no OpenCV, with Rust, Python, and WebAssembly bindings.

Guide

The mdBook: the detection pipeline, marker anatomy, target generation, and a getting-started tutorial.

API reference

Generated cargo doc for every workspace crate — the detector facade, conic/homography math, and result types.

Demo

Run the detector in your browser via WebAssembly. Load a sample image, tune the config live, and watch markers decode to IDs.

Performance

Per-image, per-stage timing and sub-pixel accuracy on public sample boards — measured, not claimed.

Source on GitHub · vitavision.dev
